pr.title: Clean up `AnimId` names and rename to "submotion"
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Large naming cleanup across fighter motion-state tables and forward headers: the old `AnimId` enum/type terminology was renamed to `Submotion`, with enum constants shortened from `*_AnimId_*` to `*_SM_*`. The PR body says names were checked against fighter `.dat` strings, HAL naming was preferred over peppi/community conventions when available, and duplicate reused animation names were disambiguated with numeric suffixes starting at 0. It also made a small typed API cleanup for CLink item creation by changing `it_802C8B28` from `void*` to `HSD_GObj*`, updated related local variables, removed an unnecessary `platform.h` include, and deleted a leading blank line in `tools/vimrc`.
what_changed: Renamed fighter subaction ID enums from `*_AnimId` to `*_Submotion`, for example `typedef enum ftCa_AnimId` became `typedef enum ftCa_Submotion`.

Renamed enum constants from `*_AnimId_*` to short `*_SM_*`, for example `ftCo_AnimId_None` became `ftCo_SM_None`, `ftCo_AnimId_Count` became `ftCo_SM_Count`, and `ftCa_AnimId_SwordSwing4` became `ftCa_SM_SwordSwing4`.

Updated common and character-specific motion-state tables to use the new `*_SM_*` constants, including `ftData_MotionStateList` in `src/melee/ft/ftdata3.c` and per-character `*_Init_MotionStateTable` arrays.

Changed many names to better match fighter `.dat` strings and HAL motion naming rather than older peppi/community action-state names; examples in the diff include Link/Young Link names such as `SpecialNCharge` to `SpecialNStart`, `SpecialNFullyCharged` to `SpecialNLoop`, `SpecialNFire` to `SpecialNEnd`, and `Zair` to `AirCatch`.
Introduced numeric suffixes for duplicated/reused submotion names, with evidence from the PR body and examples such as `ftCa_SM_SpecialHiThrow0` and `ftCa_SM_SpecialHiThrow1`.
Adjusted CLink appeal code around `it_802C8B28`: local `void* temp_r3` became a typed `HSD_GObj* item_gobj`, and the item pointer assignment now stores that typed object pointer.
Updated item function prototypes in `src/melee/it/it_27CF.h`: `it_802C8B28` now returns `HSD_GObj*` instead of `void*`, and `it_802C8C34` now takes `HSD_GObj*` instead of `u32`.
Removed a leading blank line from `tools/vimrc`.
smart_moves: The rename fixes a misleading term: the PR body explicitly notes these IDs are not actual animation IDs, because animations have their own IDs; `Submotion`/`SM` is a better semantic fit.
The author grounded the naming pass in fighter `.dat` strings and HAL conventions instead of only inheriting peppi or community terminology.
Using `Submotion` as the long name and `SM` as the short prefix keeps enum names compact while still communicating that these are motion/subaction identifiers.
Duplicate reused animations were handled systematically by appending numeric suffixes starting from 0, avoiding ambiguous duplicate names while preserving source-derived names.
The type cleanup in `it_802C8B28` removed a `void*` in favor of `HSD_GObj*`, making CLink item-spawn code clearer without changing the apparent control flow.
decomp_lessons: When a community term is known to be technically wrong, preserve searchability but switch code identifiers toward a term that matches observed game terminology; here, community `subaction`/old `AnimId` became `Submotion`/`SM`.
For action/submotion enum naming, compare against strings in fighter `.dat` files before relying on external libraries or community names.
HAL naming should be preferred when strings or other game evidence exist, but the PR body records that no official string was found for the general category name itself.
Do broad mechanical renames through both headers and motion-state table initializers together so enum definitions and data tables stay synchronized.
When animations are reused by multiple submotions, do not pretend the animation uniquely identifies the state; use a stable disambiguation scheme such as suffixes `0`, `1`, etc.
Small type corrections found during naming cleanup are worth capturing when they improve function signatures, such as replacing `void*`/`u32` with `HSD_GObj*` for item object pointers.
naming_conventions: `AnimId` type names should become `Submotion`, e.g. `ftCo_AnimId` to `ftCo_Submotion` and `ftCa_AnimId` to `ftCa_Submotion`.
`AnimId` enum constants should use the short `SM` infix, e.g. `ftCo_SM_None`, `ftCo_SM_Count`, `ftCa_SM_SpecialN`.
Character-specific submotion enums begin at `ftCo_SM_Count`, preserving the common-fighter enum range before character-specific entries.
Use HAL/fighter `.dat` motion strings where available, even if they differ from older peppi names.
For duplicated submotion names caused by animation reuse, append numeric suffixes starting at `0`, as in `SpecialHiThrow0` and `SpecialHiThrow1`.
Prefer phase names like `Start`, `Loop`, and `End` when supported by source strings, as shown by Link/Young Link special-N renames.
Use `AirCatch`/`AirCatchHit` style names for Zair-related submotions where the `.dat`/HAL naming evidence supports that terminology.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR is primarily a naming/type cleanup; no explicit assembly matching trick or nonmatching-to-matching tactic was documented.

The CLink appeal local-variable cleanup preserved a TODO about possibly needing only one `Fighter*`, indicating matching-sensitive aliasing/local variable structure may still have been constrained by the existing function.
The diff retains a `MUST_MATCH` unused stack block in `ftCl_Appeal_Anim`, so future cleanup should be careful not to remove stack-shaping code unless matching is revalidated.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use or revise `Submotion`/`SM` naming conventions.
Search for remaining `AnimId` identifiers after PR 817 to identify incomplete rename areas.
Search for duplicate submotion suffix patterns like `0` and `1` to verify consistency across fighters.
Inspect later changes to `it_802C8B28` and `it_802C8C34` for further item API typing cleanup.
Compare fighter `.dat` strings against remaining `Unk` or community-derived submotion names.
